finding: Documented 'resources' alias of 'res' errors with 'unknown command'; dispatch handles res/resources/monitor/chart/stats/cpu/mem but only res,cpu,mem are registered argparse subparsers, so argparse rejects 'resources' before dispatch.
evidence: README.md:263; __main__.py:2390; no add_parser('resources'); reproduced: pubrun resources -> exit unknown command
proposed_action: Register 'resources' as an argparse alias of 'res' (like ui aliases tui/gui), or remove the doc promise. Recommend making it work.

finding: Dispatch strings monitor/chart/stats appear only in the dispatch set and are unreachable (no parser, undocumented). Misleading scaffolding.
proposed_action: Remove monitor/chart/stats from the dispatch set.

finding: pubrun init (writes .pubrun.toml, prints guidance) is a primary first-use scenario with no test; regression (e.g. clobbering existing config) would ship silently.
proposed_action: Add init test incl. pre-existing-config safety case.

finding: SIGHUP interrupted-classification untested (only SIGINT+SIGTERM asserted); SIGKILL->crashed tested only via pubrun run exit-code path, not the real lock-file crashed classification of a SIGKILL'd tracked process (synthetic dead PID used instead).
evidence: README.md:323,331; test_signals.py:422 (SIGHUP docstring only),:250,:425; test_import_modes.py:509; test_status.py:239
proposed_action: Add SIGHUP interrupted test + real SIGKILL'd-child crashed test (skipif win32).

finding: HPC parent-child hydration (flagship cluster use case) only unit-tested; no end-to-end test running a child with PUBRUN_META_REF that asserts heavy-capture skip + parent-context stitching. Fixture tests/scripts/hpc_node.py exists for this but is unreferenced (dead).
proposed_action: Add one end-to-end HPC-hydration test; wire in or replace hpc_node.py.

finding: Two independent live tracked runs in separate processes at once (normal HPC array reality) not exercised; only thread-level singleton start() and runs-at-rest covered. Risk: race on shared listing/lock scanning.
proposed_action: Add two-concurrent-live-runs test against one output dir.

finding: docs/research-use.md says the public example workflow 'should be added under examples/ before public release'; it already exists (examples/minimal-research-workflow/, all 7 steps, has a test). Stale future tense understates the project.
proposed_action: Update research-use.md to present-tense pointing at the existing example.
